title: Phenol-based RNA extraction from polycarbonate filters
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"98nd3","text":"This protocol is an acid-phenol-based method for extracting RNA from samples collected onto polycarbonate filters.  Bead-beating is included to increase yields from difficult to lyse cells, for example, in some species of cyanobacteria.  We have used it successfully in RNA-sequencing projects involving lab cultures of cyanobacteria and from freshwater and marine environmental samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ivm9","text":"This protocol assumes that sample biomass has been appropriately collected onto polycarbonate filters and properly stored at -80 °C.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"f9rop","text":"Phenol is a very hazardous chemical.  Use extreme caution and follow recommended manufacturer and institutional safety guidelines. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"d5260","text":"frozen polycarbonate filters containing sample biomass","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bspg","text":"ice","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cs51m","text":"Solution A (lysing buffer) (0.5% SDS, 20 mM sodium acetate, 10 mM EDTA)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b3ma0","text":"Lysing Matrix E tubes (bead beating tubes)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3rjni","text":"basic phenol with 8-hydroxyquinoline (pH 7.8-8.2)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cm3jn","text":"acid phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (125:24:1; pH 4.8)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"itsp","text":"chloroform","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1slh3","text":"100% ethanol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ap3sl","text":"70% ethanol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dak3p","text":"RNase-free water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4il3v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"atb3e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3p1ap","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 700 µL of Solution A (lysing buffer) to an MP Biomedical Lysing Matrix E tube (or equivalent bead beating tube). Solution A consists of 0.5% SDS, 20 mM sodium acetate, and 10 mM EDTA in RNase-free molecular biology grade water.
Pre-chill tube containing Solution A on ice to prevent tube from overheating during bead beating.
Add sodium acetate to aqueous supernatant to a concentration of 0.3 M and mix.
Add 2-2.5x volume of ice-cold 100% ethanol and mix well.
Place in -80 °C freezer for at least 1 h, or preferably overnight.
Unfold polycarbonate filter (either frozen, or thawed but maintained on ice) and place into pre-chilled bead-beating tube.
Agitate tube on maximum speed for 40 s in Biospec Products Mini-beadbeater (or equivalent), then place on ice.
Add 500 µL basic phenol with 8-hydroxyquinoline (pH 7.8-8.2) plus 100 µL chloroform to tube.
Agitate tube on maximum speed for 40 s, then place briefly on ice to chill.
Centrifuge tube at maximum speed in benchtop centrifuge for 5 min.
Transfer as much of aqueous supernatant as possible from bead beating tube to new 2 mL centrifuge tube.
Re-extract aqueous supernatant twice with 500 µL of acid phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (125:24:1; pH 4.8).
Re-extract aqueous supernatant once with 500 µL basic phenol with 8-hydroxyquinoline plus 100 µL chloroform.
Re-extract twice with 500 µL pure chloroform to remove last traces of residual phenol.
Centrifuge at maximum speed at 0 °C for at least 30 min in refrigerated benchtop centrifuge. If you expect low yields, or wish to increase recovery, spin for 1 h at maximum.
Carefully decant ethanol supernatant without disturbing RNA pellet.
Wash pellet by adding 1 mL of ice-cold 70% ethanol and gently pipetting to dislodge RNA pellet.
Centrifuge at maximum speed at 0 °C for at least 15 min in a refrigerated benchtop centrifuge.
Gently and carefully aspirate as much of the 70% ethanol from the tube as possible taking caution not to accidently aspirate the RNA pellet.
Place the open tube in a 37 °C heating block for 3-5 min to evaporate all ethanol. Do not dry pellet to completion, as this can make RNA difficult to redissolve in water.
Dissolve the RNA pellet in an amount of RNase-free water appropriate for your expected yield and needed final concentration, typically about 50-100 µL.
creator: Robbie Martin
